title: THIS Is Why Quantum Computers Are So Hard To Make
summary: This video discusses the challenges of building quantum computers, explaining how quantum decoherence occurs when subatomic particles interact with observers, causing them to collapse from superposition states. It covers why quantum hardware requires specific, energy-intensive environments to maintain coherence.

key_claims.claim: Subatomic particles exist in a probabilistic state of many possibilities but collapse into a single state when they interact with an observer
Quantum hardware will decohere if interfered with by anything from the outside, making this the main bottleneck for building functional quantum computers
Quantum computers require very specific and energy-intensive environments to maintain coherence
All quantum computer algorithms are universal computations, meaning any algorithm can be modeled on quantum computers
Deep is initially using a quantum computer simulator instead of quantum hardware
